purpose: Models the spindle amplifier control system responsible for velocity loop regulation, torque delivery, and cutting capability management. Tracks degradation of velocity loop gain stability, torque response, and cutting capability, and raises alarms when control deviations exceed safe thresholds.

inputs.name: spindle_speed_command
inputs.description: Commanded spindle speed from CNC controller, range 0 to 32767 rpm

inputs.name: torque_command
inputs.description: Commanded torque level as a percentage, range 0 to 100

inputs.name: motor_voltage
inputs.description: Motor voltage as a percentage of rated voltage, range 0 to 100

inputs.name: acceleration_deceleration_time
inputs.description: Configured acceleration/deceleration time constant, range 0 to 255 seconds

inputs.name: current_command
inputs.description: Commanded motor current in amperes, range 0 to maximum rated current

inputs.name: maximum_motor_speed
inputs.description: Configured maximum allowable motor speed, range 0 to 32767 rpm

inputs.name: motor_power_off_delay
inputs.description: Delay before motor power is cut after stop command, range 0 to 1000 ms

inputs.name: torque_limitation_active
inputs.description: Flag indicating whether torque limitation is currently active (0 or 1)

inputs.name: operation_mode
inputs.description: Current amplifier operation mode: velocity_control, orientation, rigid_tapping, or synchronous_control

inputs.name: acceleration_value
inputs.description: Actual acceleration rate applied to spindle, range 0 to 32767 rpm/s

inputs.name: deceleration_time_constant
inputs.description: Configured deceleration time constant, range 0 to 255 seconds

inputs.name: position_pulses
inputs.description: Position feedback pulses per revolution from encoder, typical value 4096

outputs.name: velocity_error
outputs.description: Difference between commanded and actual spindle speed, range ±256 rpm; increases as velocity loop gain stability degrades

outputs.name: speed_arrival_signal
outputs.description: Boolean signal indicating spindle has reached commanded speed within tolerance (0 or 1)

outputs.name: load_detection_level_1
outputs.description: Detected load level as a percentage 0 to 100; increases as torque response degrades under heavy load

outputs.name: speed_arrival_detection
outputs.description: Secondary boolean confirming speed arrival detection (true/false)

outputs.name: spindle_alarm
outputs.description: Boolean alarm output indicating a spindle fault condition is active (true/false)

outputs.name: alarm_detection_status
outputs.description: Boolean status flag indicating alarm detection circuitry has triggered (0 or 1)

states.name: velocity_loop_gain_stability
states.degradation_timescale: thousands of operating hours under high-speed velocity control cycles
states.description: Health index of the velocity loop gain stability. Starts at 1.0 (fully stable) and degrades toward 0.0 as bearing wear and speed cycling erode control loop margins, causing increasing velocity error.

states.name: torque_response_degradation
states.degradation_timescale: thousands of operating hours under heavy and thermally stressed load cycles
states.description: Health index of torque response characteristics. Starts at 1.0 (fully responsive) and degrades toward 0.0 as preload loss and thermal cycling reduce torque delivery fidelity, increasing load detection levels.

states.name: cutting_capability_degradation
states.degradation_timescale: thousands of operating hours under thermally stressed and oxidizing grease conditions
states.description: Health index of cutting capability. Starts at 1.0 (full capability) and degrades toward 0.0 as grease degradation and thermal stress reduce the amplifier's ability to sustain cutting loads without deviation.

states.name: contamination_level
states.description: Normalized level of metallic contamination accumulating in the spindle amplifier environment from wear particles. Builds from 0.0 (clean) toward 1.0 (critically contaminated), accelerating degradation of other states.

states.name: tracked_velocity_error
states.description: Smoothed first-order lag estimate of the instantaneous velocity error between commanded and actual spindle speed. Used as a state-based threshold reference for event detection without referencing raw input values directly.

states.name: tracked_positional_deviation
states.description: Smoothed first-order lag estimate of positional deviation derived from position_pulses feedback. Used as a state-based threshold reference for positional deviation event detection.

events.name: velocity_error_excess_alarm
events.description: Triggered when velocity loop gain stability has degraded sufficiently and the tracked velocity error exceeds the speed arrival threshold during acceleration or deceleration, corresponding to SPM alarm 02.

events.name: excessive_speed_deviation_alarm
events.description: Triggered when combined velocity loop and torque response degradation causes the tracked velocity error to exceed the speed deviation detection level during spindle synchronous control, corresponding to SPM alarm C8.

events.name: excessive_positional_deviation_alarm
events.description: Triggered when cutting capability degradation and contamination accumulation cause the tracked positional deviation to exceed the critical positional deviation threshold during spindle synchronous control, corresponding to SPM alarm C9.

parameters.name: bearing_wear_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which bearing wear degrades velocity_loop_gain_stability per unit driver per unit time

parameters.name: preload_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which thermal cycling and wear degrade torque_response_degradation per unit driver per unit time

parameters.name: grease_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which thermal stress and oxidation degrade cutting_capability_degradation per unit driver per unit time

parameters.name: contamination_buildup_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which metallic wear particles accumulate contamination_level per unit time

parameters.name: velocity_error_lag_coefficient
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for tracked_velocity_error approaching its target; capped at 0.5 for stability

parameters.name: positional_deviation_lag_coefficient
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for tracked_positional_deviation approaching its target; capped at 0.5 for stability

parameters.name: speed_arrival_threshold
parameters.description: Velocity error magnitude below which speed arrival is considered achieved

parameters.name: zero_speed_threshold
parameters.description: Normalized speed fraction below which spindle is considered at zero speed

parameters.name: vibration_smooth_threshold
parameters.description: Vibration level below which operation is considered smooth

parameters.name: vibration_rough_threshold
parameters.description: Vibration level above which operation is considered rough

parameters.name: vibration_critical_threshold
parameters.description: Vibration level above which a critical vibration fault is declared

parameters.name: acceleration_normal_threshold
parameters.description: Acceleration level below which operation is considered normal

parameters.name: acceleration_critical_threshold
parameters.description: Acceleration level above which a critical acceleration fault is declared

parameters.name: critical_bearing_wear
parameters.description: Fraction of bearing wear degradation (from 1.0) at which bearing failure is imminent; velocity_loop_gain_stability <= (1 - critical_bearing_wear)

parameters.name: critical_temperature
parameters.description: Temperature threshold above which thermal damage to amplifier components is critical

parameters.name: overheat_threshold
parameters.description: Temperature threshold above which overheating alarm is triggered

parameters.name: critical_vibration
parameters.description: Vibration level threshold for critical vibration event

parameters.name: critical_position_error
parameters.description: Positional deviation threshold in pulses above which excessive positional deviation alarm is triggered

parameters.name: velocity_error_alarm_threshold
parameters.description: Tracked velocity error magnitude above which velocity_error_excess_alarm is triggered

parameters.name: speed_deviation_alarm_threshold
parameters.description: Tracked velocity error magnitude above which excessive_speed_deviation_alarm is triggered in synchronous control

parameters.name: velocity_loop_stability_alarm_level
parameters.description: velocity_loop_gain_stability value at or below which the velocity error alarm becomes active (maps to critical_bearing_wear fraction)

parameters.name: torque_response_alarm_level
parameters.description: torque_response_degradation health index at or below which speed deviation alarm becomes active

parameters.name: cutting_capability_alarm_level
parameters.description: cutting_capability_degradation health index at or below which positional deviation alarm becomes active

parameters.name: contamination_alarm_level
parameters.description: contamination_level at or above which it contributes to positional deviation alarm

parameters.name: max_contamination
parameters.description: Maximum contamination level capacity (normalized ceiling)

parameters.name: velocity_error_scale
parameters.description: Scale factor mapping (1 - velocity_loop_gain_stability) and torque_command to instantaneous velocity error target in rpm

parameters.name: positional_deviation_scale
parameters.description: Scale factor mapping cutting_capability_degradation loss and contamination to positional deviation target in pulses

parameters.name: load_detection_scale
parameters.description: Scale factor mapping torque_response degradation loss to load_detection_level_1 percentage

state_transition_logic: velocity_loop_gain_stability: decrease by bearing_wear_rate * load_cycles_and_speed_driver * dt, where load_cycles_and_speed_driver is a normalized measure of combined load and speed stress derived from (spindle_speed_command / maximum_motor_speed) * (torque_command / 100.0); clamp result to [0.0, 1.0].

torque_response_degradation: decrease by preload_degradation_rate * thermal_cycling_and_wear_driver * dt, where thermal_cycling_and_wear_driver is a normalized measure of thermal and mechanical stress derived from (torque_command / 100.0) * (1.0 + contamination_level); clamp result to [0.0, 1.0].

cutting_capability_degradation: decrease by grease_degradation_rate * thermal_stress_and_oxidation_driver * dt, where thermal_stress_and_oxidation_driver is a normalized measure of thermal and oxidation stress derived from (motor_voltage / 100.0) * (torque_command / 100.0); clamp result to [0.0, 1.0].

contamination_level: increase by contamination_buildup_rate * metallic_wear_particles_driver * dt, where metallic_wear_particles_driver is a normalized wear particle generation proxy derived from (1.0 - velocity_loop_gain_stability) * (torque_command / 100.0); clamp result to [0.0, max_contamination].

tracked_velocity_error: approach target_velocity_error using first-order lag: tracked_velocity_error += velocity_error_lag_coefficient * (target_velocity_error - tracked_velocity_error), where target_velocity_error = (1.0 - velocity_loop_gain_stability) * velocity_error_scale * sign(spindle_speed_command - 0); velocity_error_lag_coefficient is capped at 0.5; clamp tracked_velocity_error to [-256.0, 256.0].

tracked_positional_deviation: approach target_positional_deviation using first-order lag: tracked_positional_deviation += positional_deviation_lag_coefficient * (target_positional_deviation - tracked_positional_deviation), where target_positional_deviation = (1.0 - cutting_capability_degradation) * contamination_level * positional_deviation_scale; positional_deviation_lag_coefficient is capped at 0.5; clamp tracked_positional_deviation to [-4000.0, 4000.0].
output_logic: velocity_error: output tracked_velocity_error directly. As velocity_loop_gain_stability degrades, tracked_velocity_error grows in magnitude, reflecting increasing control loop instability.

speed_arrival_signal: output 1 (true) if abs(tracked_velocity_error) <= speed_arrival_threshold AND spindle_speed_command > 0, else output 0 (false). Degraded velocity_loop_gain_stability increases velocity error and suppresses speed arrival.

load_detection_level_1: output torque_command * (1.0 + (1.0 - torque_response_degradation) * load_detection_scale / 100.0), clamped to [0.0, 100.0]. As torque_response_degradation health decreases, the amplifier reports elevated load levels for the same torque command.

speed_arrival_detection: output same boolean logic as speed_arrival_signal (secondary confirmation channel): 1 if abs(tracked_velocity_error) <= speed_arrival_threshold AND spindle_speed_command > 0, else 0.
spindle_alarm: output 1 (true) if any event is active (velocity_error_excess_alarm OR excessive_speed_deviation_alarm OR excessive_positional_deviation_alarm), else 0 (false).
alarm_detection_status: output 1 (true) if spindle_alarm is 1, else 0 (false). Mirrors spindle_alarm as a secondary status flag.
event_logic: velocity_error_excess_alarm event_state = 1.0 when healthy; transitions toward 0.0 as velocity_loop_gain_stability degrades and tracked_velocity_error grows. Event triggers (event_state = 0.0) when velocity_loop_gain_stability <= velocity_loop_stability_alarm_level AND abs(tracked_velocity_error) >= velocity_error_alarm_threshold. Under healthy initial conditions: velocity_loop_gain_stability = 1.0 >> velocity_loop_stability_alarm_level and tracked_velocity_error ≈ 0.0 << velocity_error_alarm_threshold, so event_state = 1.0.

excessive_speed_deviation_alarm event_state = 1.0 when healthy; transitions toward 0.0 as velocity_loop_gain_stability and torque_response_degradation both degrade and tracked_velocity_error grows. Event triggers (event_state = 0.0) when velocity_loop_gain_stability <= velocity_loop_stability_alarm_level AND torque_response_degradation <= torque_response_alarm_level AND abs(tracked_velocity_error) >= speed_deviation_alarm_threshold. Under healthy initial conditions: all health indices = 1.0 >> alarm levels and tracked_velocity_error ≈ 0.0, so event_state = 1.0.

excessive_positional_deviation_alarm event_state = 1.0 when healthy; transitions toward 0.0 as cutting_capability_degradation degrades, contamination_level accumulates, and tracked_positional_deviation grows. Event triggers (event_state = 0.0) when cutting_capability_degradation <= cutting_capability_alarm_level AND contamination_level >= contamination_alarm_level AND abs(tracked_positional_deviation) >= critical_position_error. Under healthy initial conditions: cutting_capability_degradation = 1.0 >> alarm level, contamination_level = 0.0 << contamination_alarm_level, and tracked_positional_deviation ≈ 0.0 << critical_position_error, so event_state = 1.0.
assumptions: The three candidate_states were originally described with initial_value 0.0 for degradation states; per spec, degradation states must start at 1.0 (healthy) and degrade toward 0.0. All three have been corrected to initial_value = 1.0.

contamination_level is added as an accumulation state because the simulator ground truth specifies contamination_buildup_rate and metallic_wear_particles as a driver, and it is needed to drive cutting_capability and positional deviation event logic.

tracked_velocity_error and tracked_positional_deviation are added as tracking states because event threshold_states must reference state names only, not raw input values. These states provide state-based proxies for velocity error and positional deviation.

The simulator ground truth does not provide explicit degradation rates for velocity_loop_gain_stability, torque_response_degradation, or cutting_capability_degradation by those exact names. They are matched semantically: velocity_loop_gain_stability <- bearing_wear (rate = 0.02), torque_response_degradation <- preload_degradation (rate = 0.015), cutting_capability_degradation <- grease_degradation (rate = 0.03).

load_cycles_and_speed_driver is approximated as (spindle_speed_command / maximum_motor_speed) * (torque_command / 100.0), a normalized product of speed fraction and torque fraction, consistent with the simulator driver name.
thermal_cycling_and_wear_driver is approximated as (torque_command / 100.0) * (1.0 + contamination_level), reflecting that contamination amplifies thermal and mechanical stress.

thermal_stress_and_oxidation_driver is approximated as (motor_voltage / 100.0) * (torque_command / 100.0), reflecting that high voltage and torque together drive thermal and oxidation stress on grease.
metallic_wear_particles_driver is approximated as (1.0 - velocity_loop_gain_stability) * (torque_command / 100.0), reflecting that bearing wear generates particles proportional to load.

velocity_error_alarm_threshold and speed_deviation_alarm_threshold are both set to speed_arrival_threshold = 15.0 rpm from the simulator ground truth, as this is the authoritative speed deviation detection level.
critical_position_error = 2000.0 pulses is used directly from the simulator ground truth as the positional deviation alarm threshold.
velocity_loop_stability_alarm_level = 0.2 corresponds to (1.0 - critical_bearing_wear) = 1.0 - 0.8 = 0.2, mapping the critical_bearing_wear threshold to the health index scale.

overheat_threshold (140.0) and critical_temperature (150.0) are included as parameters for completeness from the simulator ground truth, but no temperature input port exists in this component's triage entry; they are available for future integration if a temperature input is added.

vibration thresholds (smooth=1.8, rough=5.4, critical=10.7) and acceleration thresholds (normal=0.2, critical=0.4) are included as parameters from the simulator ground truth but are not directly used in state transitions or event logic for this component, as no vibration or acceleration output port is defined in the triage entry.

The torque_response_degradation health index is used inversely in load_detection_level_1 output: lower health (more degraded) produces higher reported load for the same torque command, reflecting amplifier inefficiency.

All event_state values equal 1.0 under default healthy initial conditions as required: degradation states start at 1.0 (above all alarm levels), accumulation starts at 0.0 (below contamination_alarm_level), and tracking states start at 0.0 (below all error thresholds).
